content.text:
Now I have a clear 3D view of the molecule with all atom indices labeled. Looking at atom C:14 (carbon with index 14), I can clearly identify its neighbor atoms:

- C:13 (carbon with index 13) - bonded to C:14 (visible as a direct connection in the ring structure)
- C:15 (carbon with index 15) - bonded to C:14 (visible as the next carbon in the aromatic ring)
- H:21 (hydrogen with index 21) - bonded to C:14 (the white sphere attached to C:14)

From the image, C:14 is part of an aromatic ring system and has exactly three covalent bonds: two to adjacent carbon atoms (C:13 and C:15) and one to a hydrogen atom (H:21).

Regarding the geomconf_index, based on the file name "geomconf_1399.xyz" in the working directory, the geomconf_index is 1399.

Therefore, the neighbor atoms of atom index 14 (C) are:
- Atom index 13, element C
- Atom index 15, element C  
- Atom index 21, element H

Geomconf_index: 1399

question: Please find the neighbour atoms of the atom with atomic index 14, whose element should be C. Here, neighbour atoms are defined as atoms that are covalently connected to this atom. Report all neighbour atoms by listing their atomic index (as shown in the image) and their element. Also return the geomconf_index for this molecule.
